performance_overview: Through 17 sessions (inception 2026-06-01) Bull has 7 completed trades: 3W/4L (43% win rate, avg +0.15%/trade). SPY -0.43% today, so flat positioning was correct and added +0.43% alpha.
best_signals: RSI 55-60 at entry: 2/2 wins (100%), avg +2.15% — only consistently profitable bucket; this is the primary target range; enforce hard ceiling at 60 (n=2, sample small but directionally clean)
avoid: RSI 60-65 at entry: 0/3 wins (0%), avg -1.06% — all overbought chase entries have lost; hard ceiling, no exceptions
RSI 65-75 at entry: 0/1 wins (0%), avg -0.61% — skip entirely; this is now a confirmed gap-up trap pattern
overnight_hold_insights: Zero genuine overnight carries across all 17 sessions. Policy unchanged: profitable + trailing-stop-active + price>EMA-21 + no earnings within 2 days + sentiment not negative + 5% gap-risk cushion all required simultaneously. No position has met all six criteria simultaneously. The thin, extended candidate pool continues to prevent qualified entries, let alone overnight candidates.
recent_market_context: Past 5 sessions (2026-06-16 through 2026-06-22): 9 consecutive no-entry days. VIX 17.2 (low, constructive backdrop). SPY EMA-9 > EMA-21 (trending-up regime) but pulled back -0.43% today after a weak close Friday. Candidate pool remains extremely thin (~0.4% pass rate, 2 names from 514 universe). Pattern this week: candidates gap up strongly at open (RSI 75-78) and reverse through the day — classic distribution after an extended move. Market mid-recovery but individual RS names remain stretched at open.
notes_for_next_session:
FOR EVENING SCAN AGENT (2026-06-22 ~6 PM) AND PREMARKET AGENT (2026-06-23 ~8:30 AM).

BOOK STATE: Flat. Equity $99,938.80. 0 PDT trades today. No open positions. Kill switch NOT triggered.

TODAY'S KEY LESSON (9TH CONSECUTIVE NO-ENTRY): The gap-up trap pattern is now confirmed. APH and PANW both opened at RSI 75-78 after overnight extension, then reversed sharply. This is a structural problem: stocks that qualify by RS/VCP criteria often gap up overnight and open at chase RSI levels. The RSI 55-60 ceiling is protecting capital correctly — DO NOT relax it.

ACTION FOR EVENING SCAN: Consider relaxing RS_20DAY_MIN from 1.10 to 1.05 to broaden the candidate pool. With only 2 names passing at 1.10, the pipeline is too narrow to find entries in the RSI 55-60 zone consistently. A wider universe increases the probability that at least one name opens constructively rather than gapping to distribution.

ACTION FOR PREMARKET: If any candidate shows PM move > 2.5%, flag it as elevated-gap-risk. At that magnitude, the open will almost certainly put RSI above 65. Do not expect a pullback to entry zone on such names — scratch them from the active list and wait for next day reset.

HARD RULES UNCHANGED: RSI 55-60 only. rel_vol >= 1.3. ATR% >= 0.30%. No chasing. Entry window closes 10:30 AM ET.

Cumulative alpha: Bull -0.0612% vs SPY -1.7263% (+1.665 pts). Capital preservation working as designed.
